title: Ozoroa kwangoensis
intro: Ozoroa kwangoensis, also known as the Kwango cork tree, is a tree species within the family Anacardiaceae, native to southern Africa. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help alleviate pain and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of Ozoroa kwangoensis include triterpenoids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often prepared as a tea, infusion, or ointment, and is also used in the production of traditional remedies such as tonics and creams to treat a range of health issues.
